title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who volunteered abroad. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Julian, Marine Conservation
'I had always been drawn to the ocean, but living on a remote island in the Pacific was a different reality,' Julian explains. [51]The local community was initially wary of our presence, which I hadn't prepared for.[/51] I spent my days recording coral growth and teaching local children about plastic pollution. [53]What struck me most was how my perspective on material wealth shifted dramatically within weeks.[/53] [48]I found myself perfectly content with just two sets of clothes and a basic hut.[/48] It wasn't just about saving the reef; it was about realizing how little we actually need to be happy. Leaving was surprisingly emotional, as I’d formed bonds that felt like family.
texts.B:
### Clara, Community Teaching
Clara spent six months in a mountain village helping to establish a library. 'The altitude was punishing at first,' she admits. [47]I was actually quite startled by how quickly the morning hikes and thin air became my new normal.[/47] [56]If I hadn't had a background in basic carpentry from a summer job, I wouldn't have been able to build the actual shelving units we needed.[/56] [50]I’d initially worried that the language barrier would be an insurmountable wall, but we communicated through sketches and shared goals.[/50] The joy on the children's faces when the first crates of books arrived is an image I will carry with me forever. It was exhausting but deeply fulfilling work.
texts.C:
### Tomas, Wildlife Rehabilitation
Tomas worked at a primate sanctuary. 'It was physically demanding and often quite messy,' he laughs. [54]I’d expected the animals to be the highlight, but it was actually the dedication of the permanent local staff that left the biggest impression on me.[/54] [52]There were moments, especially during the monsoon rains, when I seriously questioned why I’d paid to work in such grueling conditions.[/52] [49]However, seeing a rescued monkey return to the wild made every mosquito bite worth it.[/49] [55]The project provided a structured routine that I found incredibly grounding after years of chaotic city living.[/55] It taught me patience and the importance of slow, steady progress in conservation efforts.
texts.D:
### Elena, Sustainable Farming
Elena joined a permaculture project. 'I wanted to learn how to grow food without chemicals,' she says. [48]I was amazed to find that I didn't miss my smartphone or the internet at all after the first few days.[/48] [50]Before going, I was concerned that my lack of physical strength would be a problem, yet I adapted well to the labor.[/50] [53]It's strange, but I now view a simple tomato as a minor miracle of nature rather than just a grocery item.[/53] [47]The transition to waking up at dawn happened almost instantly, which was a huge shock to my system.[/47] The sense of community during communal meals was the most rewarding aspect of the whole trip for me personally.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by how quickly they adapted to a new routine?
questions.explanation: Clara mentions being 'startled' by how fast the hikes became her normal, and Elena (D) mentions waking at dawn was an 'instant' transition.

questions.question: mentions a lack of desire for modern comforts or possessions?
questions.explanation: Julian (A) was content with two sets of clothes, and Elena (D) says she didn't miss her smartphone at all.

questions.question: describes a specific moment that justified the hardships faced?
questions.explanation: Tomas mentions that seeing a monkey return to the wild made every mosquito bite worth it.

questions.question: admits to having had prior doubts about their own capabilities?
questions.explanation: Clara (B) worried about the language barrier and Elena (D) worried about her lack of physical strength.

questions.question: mentions an unexpected social challenge at the start of the project?
questions.explanation: Julian mentions that the local community was 'initially wary', which he hadn't prepared for.

questions.question: confesses to having temporary second thoughts about the experience?
questions.explanation: Tomas admits there were moments when he 'seriously questioned' why he was there.

questions.question: expresses a fundamental shift in their personal values or perceptions?
questions.explanation: Julian (A) talks about his shift in perspective on wealth, and Elena (D) mentions her new view on nature.

questions.question: notes that their primary source of inspiration was not what they anticipated?
questions.explanation: Tomas expected the animals to be the highlight but was actually more impressed by the local staff.

questions.question: mentions finding comfort in the organized nature of the work?
questions.explanation: Tomas found the 'structured routine' to be 'incredibly grounding'.

questions.question: points out that a previously acquired skill proved unexpectedly useful?
questions.explanation: Clara mentions that her background in 'basic carpentry' allowed her to build shelving.
